    You can also do this by using the `==` operator and passing in a dictionary of keys to be matched. This will match the key in the text and return the key if found, or an empty string if not found. This can be used for all other matches.
To test this function:
```python
text = """Some text with some formatting"""
matches = find_matching(text)
assert matches == True
```

Here is how you can implement this function:

```python
def find_matching(text):
    pattern = r"^@\w+\s*([a-zA-Z0-9]+) {2}[a-zA-Z0-9]*$"
    matches = [match for match in re.findall(pattern, text) if match]

    return matches
```
